titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 619

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=816 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.708456+00 | translation moa=817 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.718447+00

div.p:
. A . dei Gratia Rex Scott’ . R . de Bernham’ maiori et prepositis suis de Berwic’ salutem . Mandamus uobis
et precipimus . quatinus extraneos mercatores qui uenient usque prioratum de Coldingham’ ad lanam et alias merca
turas prioris et conuentus de Coldingham emendas . nullo modo inpediatis  quin dictas mercaturas emere
et abducere possint . saluis nobis antiquis consuetudinibus nostris . Testibus . P . Comite . de Dunbar’ . Waltero .
Olifard . Iustic’ . Laodonie . apud Edinburc . xii . die Aprilis . Anno Regni . domini Regis . Vicesimo . quarto .

p.cl: Alexander by the grace of God king of the Scots to Robert of Bernham his mayor and provost of Berwick greeting.

We command you and enjoin, that you by no means hinder foreign merchants who come to the priory of Coldingham for buying the wool and other merchandise of the prior and convent of Coldingham, but that they should be able to buy and transport the said merchandise, reserving to ourselves our ancient customs.
As witnesses, Patrick earl of Dunbar, Walter Oliphant, justiciar of Lothian,
at Edinburgh, the 12th day of April, in the year of the reign of the lord king, the twenty-fourth [1238].
